report.executive_summary: TITLE COMMITMENT 01-26006219-01T is NOT READY FOR CLOSING. This transaction presents critical defects that prevent policy issuance and must be resolved immediately. The most severe issue is Requirement 4: the recorded deed (Instrument #20260174, recorded 01/13/2026) names Christopher Lane Thornhill and Sara E. Jackson as grantees instead of the proposed buyer Kyla B. Fortenberry, creating a complete chain of title break. Additionally, a fundamental discrepancy exists between the vested owner (GARNER TREE FARM, LLC) and the seller (Terri G. Crawley individually), requiring submission of LLC organizational documentation and member authorization. The commitment contains duplicate mineral interest exceptions requiring consolidation, and the deed of trust from buyer to Society Mortgage remains unrecorded. With a readiness score of 67/100 and three open flags (one critical, one high, one medium), closing cannot proceed until the grantee discrepancy is corrected through a new recorded deed and all LLC compliance documentation is provided and approved by underwriting.

report.requirements:
REQUIREMENT 1 - VESTED OWNER/SELLER DISCREPANCY [OPEN - HIGH PRIORITY]
Status: NOT SATISFIED
Description: Vested Owner is GARNER TREE FARM, LLC; Seller is listed as TERRI G. CRAWLEY individually
Action Required: Advise customer service of circumstances explaining discrepancy and obtain clarification on whether Terri G. Crawley is authorized to convey on behalf of the LLC or if the LLC must be the conveying party
Underwriting Impact: Fundamental title defect requiring resolution before closing

REQUIREMENT 2 - VERIFY OPEN DEED OF TRUST [OPEN]
Status: PENDING VERIFICATION
Description: No open deed of trust found in Marion County records
Action Required: Verify by inquiry of escrow personnel and/or proposed insured regarding any existing liens or encumbrances
Underwriting Impact: Standard verification requirement

REQUIREMENT 3 - BANKRUPTCY SEARCH [SATISFIED]
Status: COMPLETED
Description: No bankruptcy proceedings found for GARNER TREE FARM, LLC, TERRI G. CRAWLEY, and KYLA B. FORTENBERRY
Underwriting Impact: Clear search results

REQUIREMENT 4 - RECORD CORRECTED DEED [OPEN - CRITICAL]
Status: NOT SATISFIED - CRITICAL DEFECT
Description: Requirement mandates recording a deed from GARNER TREE FARM, LLC to KYLA B. FORTENBERRY
Current Status: Deed recorded 01/13/2026 (Instrument #20260174) names Christopher Lane Thornhill and Sara E. Jackson as grantees instead of Kyla B. Fortenberry
Action Required: IMMEDIATE - Obtain corrected warranty deed from GARNER TREE FARM, LLC naming KYLA B. FORTENBERRY as sole grantee and record in Marion County records. Current deed must be addressed (potential need for correction deed or reconveyance).
Underwriting Impact: CRITICAL - Prevents issuance of owner's policy; creates chain of title break; transaction cannot close without resolution

REQUIREMENT 5 - RECORD DEED OF TRUST [OPEN]
Status: NOT SATISFIED
Description: Record satisfactory executed Deed of Trust from KYLA B. FORTENBERRY as Trustor, in favor of SOCIETY MORTGAGE as Beneficiary, securing original principal balance of $80,808.00
Action Required: Obtain executed deed of trust and record in Marion County records
Underwriting Impact: Required for lender's policy issuance; dependent on Requirement 4 resolution

REQUIREMENT 6 - LLC DOCUMENTATION AND COMPLIANCE [OPEN - HIGH PRIORITY]
Status: NOT SATISFIED
Description: Submit: (a) Copy of operating agreement and amendments or certification of none; (b) Evidence LLC is in good standing; (c) Resolution signed by all members authorizing transaction; (d) Other requirements following review of organizational documents
Action Required: IMMEDIATE - Obtain and submit:
  - Current operating agreement for GARNER TREE FARM, LLC with all amendments
  - Certificate of Good Standing from Mississippi Secretary of State
  - Member Authorization Resolution signed by all four members (Ricky Lane McPhail, Terri Garner Crawley, Patsy Garner Brewton, Karen Garner Delancey) authorizing the sale to Kyla B. Fortenberry
  - Any additional documentation required following underwriting review
Underwriting Impact: HIGH - Cannot confirm LLC's authority to convey without documentation; must verify all members consent to transaction

report.risk_flags:
CRITICAL RISK FLAG - REQUIREMENT 4 NOT SATISFIED: WRONG GRANTEE IN RECORDED DEED
Severity: CRITICAL
Status: OPEN
Description: Requirement 4 mandates recording a deed from GARNER TREE FARM, LLC to KYLA B. FORTENBERRY. However, the recorded deed (Instrument #20260174, recorded 01/13/2026) conveys the property to Christopher Lane Thornhill and Sara E. Jackson instead.
Impact Analysis:
  - Complete failure to satisfy Requirement 4
  - Creates a chain of title break that prevents issuance of owner's policy to Kyla B. Fortenberry
  - Title does not vest in proposed insured; transaction cannot close
  - Raises questions about transaction legitimacy and whether wrong parties were named in deed
  - May require corrective deed, reconveyance, or complete re-execution of transaction
Remediation Required: IMMEDIATE
  - Obtain corrected warranty deed from GARNER TREE FARM, LLC naming KYLA B. FORTENBERRY as sole grantee
  - Record corrected deed in Marion County records
  - Obtain title commitment amendment reflecting corrected chain of title
  - Underwriting approval required before proceeding
Closing Impact: TRANSACTION CANNOT CLOSE - This is a showstopper defect

HIGH RISK FLAG - MINERAL RESERVATIONS AND OIL/GAS INTERESTS: DUPLICATE EXCEPTIONS
Severity: HIGH
Status: OPEN
Description: Exceptions 1 and 8 both address mineral interests, creating ambiguity and redundancy. Exception 1 covers "any prior reservation or conveyance of minerals of every kind and character, including oil, gas, sand and gravel." Exception 8 covers "oil, gas, coal and other mineral interests." The warranty deed confirms minerals are severed: "LESS AND EXCEPT all oil, gas, and other minerals in, on, and under all of the lands herein conveyed."
Impact Analysis:
  - Overlapping exceptions create confusion about actual mineral rights status
  - Buyer receives property without mineral rights (severed from conveyance)
  - Duplicate exceptions reduce clarity in final policy
  - Mineral severance is confirmed and acceptable, but presentation is deficient
Remediation Required:
  - Consolidate Exceptions 1 and 8 into single, clear exception in final policy
  - Confirm with buyer that mineral rights are not included in purchase
  - Ensure final policy clearly states minerals are reserved/severed
Closing Impact: MODERATE - Does not prevent closing but requires exception consolidation before policy issuance

MEDIUM RISK FLAG - VESTED OWNER VS. SELLER DISCREPANCY: LLC VS. INDIVIDUAL
Severity: MEDIUM/HIGH
Status: OPEN
Description: Commitment shows GARNER TREE FARM, LLC as vested owner, but TERRI G. CRAWLEY is listed as seller individually. This fundamental discrepancy must be resolved. The deed recorded 01/13/2026 shows GARNER TREE FARM, LLC as grantor (correct), but conveys to wrong grantees (Christopher Lane Thornhill and Sara E. Jackson).
Impact Analysis:
  - Unclear whether Terri G. Crawley has authority to convey on behalf of LLC
  - LLC has four members; unclear if all members consent to sale
  - Potential for member disputes or claims against title
  - Requirement 6 documentation not yet submitted
  - Creates title defect if LLC authority is not properly established
Remediation Required: IMMEDIATE
  - Obtain and submit LLC operating agreement and all amendments
  - Obtain Certificate of Good Standing from Mississippi Secretary of State
  - Obtain Member Authorization Resolution signed by all four members:
    * Ricky Lane McPhail
    * Terri Garner Crawley
    * Patsy Garner Brewton
    * Karen Garner Delancey
  - Clarify whether Terri G. Crawley is authorized representative or if LLC must execute documents
  - Underwriting review and approval required
Closing Impact: PREVENTS CLOSING - Cannot issue policy without confirmation of LLC authority and member consent

SUMMARY OF OPEN FLAGS:
- 1 CRITICAL flag (Requirement 4 - Wrong Grantee)
- 1 HIGH flag (Duplicate Mineral Exceptions)
- 1 MEDIUM/HIGH flag (LLC Authority and Member Consent)
Total Open Flags: 3
Flags Preventing Closing: 2 (Critical and Medium/High)
